2605.02658.title: Deciphering Shortcut Learning from an Evolutionary Game Theory Perspective
2605.02658.paper_summary: Shortcut learning causes deep learning models to rely on non-essential features within the data. However, its formation in deep neural network training still lacks theoretical understanding. In this paper, we provide a formal definition of core and shortcut features and employ evolutionary game theory to analyze the origins of shortcut bias by modeling data samples as players and their corresponding neural tangent features as strategies, assuming the existence of core and shortcut subnetworks. We find that gradient descent (GD) and stochastic gradient descent (SGD) lead to two distinct stochastically stable states, each corresponding to a different strategy. The former primarily optimizes the shortcut subnetwork, while the latter primarily optimizes the core subnetwork. We investigate the influence of these strategies on shortcut bias through a continuous stochastic differential equation, and reveal the impact of data noise and optimization noise on the formation of shortcut bias. In brief, our work employs evolutionary game theory to characterize the dynamics of shortcut bias formation and provides a theoretical view on its mitigation.
2605.02658.paper_summary_zh: 快捷學習使深度學習模型依賴於數據中的非必要特徵。<lb/>然而，在深度神經網絡訓練中，其形成仍缺乏理論理解。<lb/>在本文中，我們提供了核心特徵和快捷特徵的正式定義，並利用演化博弈論分析快捷偏差的起源，通過將數據樣本建模為玩家，將其對應的神經切線特徵建模為策略，假設存在核心和快捷子網絡。<lb/>我們發現，梯度下降（GD）和隨機梯度下降（SGD）導致兩種不同的隨機穩定狀態，每種狀態對應於不同的策略。<lb/>前者主要優化快捷子網絡，而後者主要優化核心子網絡。<lb/>我們通過連續隨機微分方程研究這些策略對快捷偏差的影響，並揭示數據噪聲和優化噪聲對快捷偏差形成的影響。<lb/>簡而言之，我們的工作利用演化博弈論來描述快捷偏差形成的動態，並提供了對其減緩的理論觀點。
2605.02658.author: Xiayang Li et.al.
2605.02658.authors: Xiayang Li, Kuo Gai, Shihua Zhang
